title: Impetigo
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Impetigo is a contagious bacterial skin infection caused by staph or strep bacteria.
It often starts after a break in the skin and can cause red or pimple-like sores that form a thick crust.
Scratching can spread impetigo, and antibiotics may be used for treatment.
Patients should seek care if infection spreads, fever develops, or symptoms worsen.
